subject: Re: Fwd: Please Respond
body:
darren, I didn't know anything about this. does george.. ?.. who's decision was it to have a business?. certainly not mine.. 


----- Original Message ----
From: "DKIESQ@aol.com" <DKIESQ@aol.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Friday, July 13, 2007 5:02:16 PM
Subject: Fwd: Please Respond


Please note.
 
Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
4th Floor
New York, New York 10022
Tel: 212-750-1176
Fax: 212-750-0381
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com
 
------------------------------

Unless otherwise specifically indicated above, you should assume that any statement in this email relating to any U.S. federal, state, or local tax matter was written in connection with the promotion or marketing by other parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed in this email. Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's particular circumstances from an independent tax advisor. 
================================================================= 
Confidentiality Notice: This communication and any attachments contain information from Darren K. Indyke, Esq. that constitute attorney work product or that are otherwise confidential and/or privileged. Except for personal use by the intended recipient, or as expressly authorized by the sender, any person who receives this information is prohibited from disclosing, copying, distributing, and/or using it. If you have received this communication in error, please immediately delete it and all copies, and promptly notify the sender at the above telephone number or electronic mail address. Nothing in this communication is intended to operate as an electronic signature under applicable law.
=================================================================






Get a sneak peak of the all-new AOL.com.
Dear Darren,

Her Majesty's Revenue & Customs (HMRC) who have responsibility for VAT collection carry out regular reviews of all businesses who are VAT registered. In many cases, and for Foxcote, it has been on a three/four year cycle and they carried out their latest inspection last week. This is quite normal.

We are required to provide the full financial records, ie in and out invoices and VAT returns and calculations for the three years prior to their inspection. This we did.

From the review provided at the end of the day the inpsection passed successfully, without any errors or omissions being highlighted which would give concern - ie the job we are doing is being done properly.

The Inspector asked me at the end of the day why had the shoot (as opposed to any other area of business at Foxcote) suffered year on year trading loss. He said he did not expect  me to answer the question and he would write to me so that we may consider his question and then repsond. He said he did not know what he would be able to do about our answer when we did respond, but he felt he should still ask the question.

They could allege (and I am not really qualified to predit the consequences) that it is not a business. The consequence could be that we lose our VAT staus and the ability to reclaim (for Foxcote Corporation) VAT on expenditure.

HMRC have not yet written to me and when they do the letter must be sent to Saffery Champness, Macfarlanes and yourself to review.

We may well find that we can construct an appropriate answer that means this line of enquiry can go no further, however I am sure we all can see the nature of the enquiry and there exists the potential that we may not end it satisfactorily.

If you need to speak my mobile is 00 44 775 562067 and home is 00 44 1865 391511.

Regards
Mark


Mark Charter BSc MRICS FAAV
Partner
For and on behalf of Carter Jonas LLP
Tel: 01865 404406
Fax: 01865 310653
Mob: 07775 562067
